question.content: As shown, a homogeneous sphere of radius r and refractive index n is placed in vacuum, where n_0 is the refractive index of vacuum and n > n_0. A narrow laser beam of frequency nu travels along the straight line BC in vacuum, and the distance from line BC to the sphere center O is l with l < r. The beam enters the sphere at point C by refraction, propagates through the sphere, and exits into vacuum again at point D by refraction. Assume that the frequency of the laser beam remains unchanged after both refractions. Determine the magnitude of the average force exerted on the sphere by a single photon during the two refraction processes. Answer format: a single algebraic expression for F_avg; write only the expression, with no explanatory text or equals sign.

step_1.content: Use photon momentum change at two refractions on the spherical dielectric interface.
step_1.key_point: Force equals momentum-transfer rate to the sphere.

step_3.content: Combine entry and exit refraction contributions for one photon path and average.
